_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PANEL
name: Teamspeak 6 Manager
author: snxrcsme@gmail.com
description: Web-based management interface for TeamSpeak 6 servers by clusterzx. Includes backend API, React frontend (served via internal proxy), and Go WebRTC sidecar for video streaming. All three services are started together using a supervised launcher.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# TS6-Manager Pelican install script
# Clones the repo, builds all three services, and writes a start.sh + proxy.cjs.

REPO_URL="https://github.com/clusterzx/ts6-manager.git"
BRANCH="${BRANCH:-main}"
TARGET="/mnt/server"

apt-get update
apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git curl ca-certificates xz-utils build-essential golang-go ffmpeg python3 python3-pip

# Install pnpm globally
npm install -g pnpm@9 >/dev/null 2>&1 || npm install -g pnpm@9

cd "$TARGET"

if [ -d ".git" ]; then
    echo "[install] Existing repo, pulling latest..."
    git fetch --all
    git checkout "$BRANCH"
    git pull
else
    echo "[install] Cloning $REPO_URL (branch $BRANCH)..."
    git clone --branch "$BRANCH" "$REPO_URL" src_clone
    shopt -s dotglob
    mv src_clone/* . 2>/dev/null || true
    mv src_clone/.[!.]* . 2>/dev/null || true
    rmdir src_clone 2>/dev/null || true
fi

echo "[install] Installing node dependencies with pnpm..."
pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile

echo "[install] Building @ts6/common..."
pnpm --filter @ts6/common run build

echo "[install] Running prisma generate..."
pnpm --filter @ts6/backend exec prisma generate

echo "[install] Building backend..."
pnpm --filter @ts6/backend run build

echo "[install] Building frontend..."
pnpm --filter @ts6/frontend run build

echo "[install] Building Go sidecar..."
cd packages/sidecar
CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -ldflags='-s -w' -o "$TARGET/bin/sidecar" main.go
cd "$TARGET"
chmod +x bin/sidecar

echo "[install] Fetching yt-dlp static binary..."
mkdir -p bin
curl -L -o bin/yt-dlp https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/releases/latest/download/yt-dlp_linux
chmod +x bin/yt-dlp

echo "[install] Installing small runtime deps for the internal static+proxy server..."
pnpm add --ignore-workspace -w express http-proxy-middleware 2>/dev/null || npm install --prefix . express http-proxy-middleware

echo "[install] Creating directories for persistent data..."
mkdir -p packages/backend/data /data/music

# Generate .env if missing (first install only)
if [ ! -f .env ]; then
    echo "[install] Generating .env with fresh secrets..."
    JWT_SECRET_VAL=$(head -c 48 /dev/urandom | base64 | tr -d '=+/' | head -c 48)
    ENCRYPTION_KEY_VAL=$(head -c 48 /dev/urandom | base64 | tr -d '=+/' | head -c 48)
    cat > .env <<EOF
NODE_ENV=production
JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET_VAL}
ENCRYPTION_KEY=${ENCRYPTION_KEY_VAL}
JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRY=15m
JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRY=7d
BACKEND_PORT=3001
SIDECAR_PORT=9800
DATABASE_URL=file:./data/ts6webui.db
MUSIC_DIR=/home/container/data/music
SIDECAR_URL=http://127.0.0.1:9800
PATH_BIN=/home/container/bin
EOF
fi

# Write start.sh
cat > start.sh <<'STARTSH'
#!/bin/bash
set -u

cd /home/container

# Load .env
if [ -f .env ]; then
    set -a
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    . ./.env
    set +a
fi

export PATH="/home/container/bin:${PATH}"
export BACKEND_PORT="${BACKEND_PORT:-3001}"
export SIDECAR_PORT="${SIDECAR_PORT:-9800}"
export SIDECAR_URL="http://127.0.0.1:${SIDECAR_PORT}"
export FRONTEND_URL="${FRONTEND_URL:-http://0.0.0.0:${SERVER_PORT}}"
export DATABASE_URL="${DATABASE_URL:-file:./data/ts6webui.db}"
export MUSIC_DIR="${MUSIC_DIR:-/home/container/data/music}"
export PORT="${BACKEND_PORT}"

mkdir -p packages/backend/data "$MUSIC_DIR"

echo "[start] Running prisma db push (idempotent)..."
( cd packages/backend && npx prisma db push --skip-generate || true )

echo "[start] Launching Go sidecar on :${SIDECAR_PORT}..."
SIDECAR_PORT="$SIDECAR_PORT" /home/container/bin/sidecar &
SIDECAR_PID=$!

echo "[start] Launching backend on :${BACKEND_PORT}..."
( cd packages/backend && node dist/index.js ) &
BACKEND_PID=$!

# Wait until backend is listening
for _ in $(seq 1 30); do
    if (echo > /dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/"$BACKEND_PORT") 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "[start] Backend up on :$BACKEND_PORT"
        break
    fi
    sleep 1
done

# Cleanup on exit
trap 'kill -TERM $SIDECAR_PID $BACKEND_PID 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0' INT TERM

echo "[start] Starting frontend static + API proxy on :${SERVER_PORT}..."
echo "TS6-Manager ready on port ${SERVER_PORT}"
exec node proxy.cjs
STARTSH
chmod +x start.sh

# Write proxy.cjs: serves the built frontend and proxies /api + /ws to the backend.
cat > proxy.cjs <<'PROXYJS'
const path = require('path');
const express = require('express');
const { createProxyMiddleware } = require('http-proxy-middleware');

const PORT = parseInt(process.env.SERVER_PORT || '3000', 10);
const BACKEND = `http://127.0.0.1:${process.env.BACKEND_PORT || '3001'}`;
const STATIC_DIR = path.resolve(__dirname, 'packages/frontend/dist');

const app = express();

app.use('/api', createProxyMiddleware({
  target: BACKEND,
  changeOrigin: true,
  ws: true,
  xfwd: true,
}));

app.use('/ws', createProxyMiddleware({
  target: BACKEND,
  changeOrigin: true,
  ws: true,
  xfwd: true,
}));

app.use(express.static(STATIC_DIR, { index: 'index.html' }));

// SPA fallback
app.get('*', (req, res) => {
  res.sendFile(path.join(STATIC_DIR, 'index.html'));
});

const server = app.listen(PORT, '0.0.0.0', () => {
  console.log(`[proxy] Frontend+API on 0.0.0.0:${PORT} -> backend ${BACKEND}`);
});

// WebSocket upgrade pass-through to backend
server.on('upgrade', (req, socket, head) => {
  // http-proxy-middleware handles /api and /ws upgrades via the app chain
});
PROXYJS

echo "[install] Done."

variables.name: Git Branch
variables.description: Git branch, tag, or commit to check out when installing (default: main).

variables.name: Backend Port
variables.description: Internal port the Node.js backend listens on. Stays inside the container, not publicly reachable. Change only if it collides with something else.

variables.name: Sidecar Port
variables.description: Internal port the Go WebRTC sidecar listens on. Stays inside the container.

variables.name: JWT Secret
variables.description: Secret used to sign JWTs. Auto-generated on first install; rotate to invalidate all tokens.

variables.name: Encryption Key
variables.description: Dedicated AES-256-GCM key for stored credential encryption (API keys, SSH passwords). Auto-generated on install. If unset, backend falls back to JWT_SECRET.

variables.name: Frontend URL (CORS origin)
variables.description: Public URL users will open in their browser. Used as CORS origin on the backend. Example: https://ts6.example.com

variables.name: Allow Self-Signed TS Certs
variables.description: Set to true if your TeamSpeak WebQuery uses a self-signed certificate. Default: false.
